data.description:
## Why
Config resolution is on the COMMAND HOT PATH — every CLI invocation + many library reads resolve config. The new typed Config (tomllib parse + upward project discovery + XDG fallback + CLI/env/file layering) must not regress vs the bespoke flat-file parser it replaces. Discovery walks and TOML parsing are the main risk.

## What
Benchmark config resolution and prove it is fast + bounded + cached.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Microbenchmark: resolve config (cold + warm) across the layers; record p50/p95 vs the current inline-parser baseline
- [ ] Config is parsed/discovered ONCE and cached per process/root (not re-read per config access); assert no per-read re-parse or repeated upward directory walk
- [ ] Upward discovery walk is bounded (stops at .git/fs-root) and cheap; no unbounded stat storms on deep trees
- [ ] No measurable regression on the command hot path (e.g. `rebar list`/`show` resolve config within the baseline envelope)
- [ ] A perf regression guard test (asserts resolution stays under a budget / does not re-parse)
data.title: Performance validation for the new config functionality
